sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Benfica, sitting at 2nd in the league with 78 points, host AVS - Futebol SAD, who are struggling in 17th place with just 24 points in Round 31 at the iconic Estádio da Luz in Lisbon. This matchup pits a title-contending side against a team fighting for survival. The referee, Carlos Macedo, oversees the game with an average of 4.9 yellow cards per match, suggesting a potentially physical encounter. Benfica will look to capitalize on their superior league standing and home advantage. Meanwhile, AVS - Futebol SAD will aim to pull off an upset to boost their relegation battle.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last encounter on December 15, 2024, AVS - Futebol SAD hosted Benfica, and the match ended in a 1-1 draw, with Benfica’s Zeki Amdouni earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.0 Sofascore rating. Over the last 5 matchups between these teams, Benfica has secured 1 win, there has been 1 draw, and AVS has yet to claim a victory. Benfica’s Orkun Kökçü has stood out in these games, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.43 with 1 assist despite starting only once. Historical data suggests Benfica holds a slight edge, but AVS has shown they can compete.

sections.subtitle: Benfica’s Form and Key Players
sections.text: Benfica enters this match in fine form, with an ongoing streak of no losses in their last 8 games and scoring first in their last 8 matches. Their attacking prowess is evident with 82 goals this season, ranking 2nd in the league, and creating 136 big chances, also 1st in the league. Standout performers include Vangelis Pavlidis, 2nd in the league with 18 goals, and Kerem Aktürkoğlu, 2nd in assists with 9 and 1st in big chances created with 17. Orkun Kökçü, ranked 3rd in league average rating at 7.54 and 2nd in key passes with 71, is another key figure. Defensively, Nicolàs Otamendi impresses with a 75.7% aerial duel win rate, ranking 5th league-wide. Players like Bruma, with 2.3 successful dribbles per game (2nd in the league), add flair to their attack.

sections.subtitle: AVS - Futebol SAD’s Struggles and Standouts
sections.text: AVS - Futebol SAD is in poor form, winless in their last 6 matches and without a clean sheet in the same span, conceding first in 5 of their last 6 games. Their offensive output is limited, with Nenê leading the team with just 4 goals this season. Lucas Piazón offers some creativity with 3 assists and a team-high average rating of 7.03, though he lacks goals. Defensively, players like Kiki Afonso, with 2 assists, and Aderllan Santos, with a decent 6.89 rating, will need to step up against Benfica’s potent attack. AVS’s inability to keep games low-scoring could spell trouble at Estádio da Luz.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Benfica dominates key statistical categories, boasting the league’s best defense with only 26 goals conceded and leading in clean sheets with 15. They also excel in possession with 58.25% (3rd in the league), create 7.125 corners per game (1st), and win 55.4% of aerial duels (1st). Their goal distribution shows a strong finish, with 30.8% of goals scored between the 76th and 90th minutes. Conversely, AVS - Futebol SAD struggles to convert chances, with their goal distribution peaking at 22.8% between the 31st and 45th minutes. AVS’s defensive frailties are evident as they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet in recent outings, and their corner stats suggest limited attacking threat, with games often under 10.5 corners in their last 10 matches.

sections.subtitle: In-Form Players to Watch
sections.text: Benfica’s recent form is boosted by players like Vangelis Pavlidis, who has scored 7 goals in his last 5 matches with an average Sofascore rating of 8.16. Kerem Aktürkoğlu also shines with 2 goals and 2 assists in the same span, alongside Orkun Kökçü, who contributed 1 goal and 2 assists. Emerging talent Gianluca Prestianni has impressed with an 8.15 rating over the last 5 games. Unfortunately, AVS - Futebol SAD lacks in-form players based on recent performances, which could hinder their ability to match Benfica’s intensity. Their reliance on players like Nenê and Lucas Piazón will be critical to avoid being overrun.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Streaks
sections.text: Benfica has an incredible streak of games with over 2.5 goals in their last 9 matches, showcasing their attacking consistency. They’ve also been the first-half winner in 7 of their last 8 games, often taking early control. AVS - Futebol SAD, on the other hand, has struggled defensively, conceding first in 5 of their last 6 matches. Benfica’s league-leading stats in tackles (18.3 per game), interceptions (9.2 per game), and penalty area entries (35.8 per game) highlight their dominance. An intriguing fact is Benfica’s 16 woodwork hits this season, ranking 2nd in the league for unluckiest teams, suggesting they could have scored even more.
